tweets.text:
Most brands are losing 6 figs a month because they're too slow.

By the time ur creative gets approved:

The trend already peaked
CPMs doubled
Ur competitor ripped the angle and scaled it

Ship fast or watch someone else win with ur idea.

One cracked UGC creator is worth more than:

> Hiring another $30k/mo agency
> Bringing on a new media buyer
> Throwing 10% more at Meta

Creative is the entire game but ur still in Slack asking if u should test manual vs auto bidding.

A lot of brands are hitting revenue goals while bleeding out.

3.4 ROAS looks sexy in the screenshot.

But:

Refund rate is 12%
NC CPA doubled in 6 weeks
U owe suppliers $80k and have $9k in the bank

Revenue is a vanity metric if ur account says otherwise.

tweets.text:
100% agree with this take. Nearly everyone shipping AI products is overpromising, overhyping, over-marketing and underdelivering. 

And a lot of these products are really good, but I’ve yet to test any that genuinely live up to the marketing hype.

Saying NO to the wrong deals and opportunities is one of the hardest, but most valuable, skills an entrepreneur can develop. 

When you are first getting started you say yes to anything and everything out of necessity, but there’s a tipping point where taking on wrong clients

One of the weirdest most cringe things (as the kids say I think) I see here, out of a pretty big list, is people quote tweeting their own tweets like it’s a brilliant tweet another person made. 

It’s like giving a big speech and quoting yourself in the speech.
